commands.review_summary:
The PR normalizes nullable and literal-union runtime tool schemas before provider exposure, preserves raw null validation, isolates cron schema instances, and refreshes affected prompt snapshots.

PR surface: Source +106, Tests +129. Total +235 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the old union-coercion path could coerce explicit null clear sentinels before validation, and the projection path exposed composition/null keywords that the new tests cover; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair is needed; the protected maintainer-labelled core agent/provider schema PR should be handled by maintainers.

target.title: fix(agents): project nullable tool schemas for providers
target.body:
## Summary

- Project nullable and literal-union tool schemas into the OpenAPI-friendly provider schema shape used for runtime tools.
- Preserve runtime validation semantics for explicit `null` clear sentinels before provider projection.
- Keep cron tool schema instances isolated so provider projection/normalization cannot mutate the raw schema used by validation or later tool instances.
- Refresh the prompt snapshots affected by the projected schema output.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check` under Node 24.15.0
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/codex-dynamic-tools.discord-group.json test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/codex-dynamic-tools.heartbeat-turn.json test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/codex-dynamic-tools.telegram-direct.json test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/discord-group-codex-message-tool.md test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/telegram-direct-codex-message-tool.md test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/telegram-heartbeat-codex-tool.md`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_cbb1c631efeb`, run `run_4415ced3845b`, command `pnpm check:changed`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, exit 0.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: strict provider adapters should not receive `anyOf`/`oneOf`/`const`/null-type composition from runtime tool schemas when a provider-safe projection is available; runtime validation should still preserve explicit `null` clear sentinels; provider projection must not poison shared cron schema objects.

Real environment tested: local Codex worktree for focused Vitest/format/lint/snapshot proof, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate from the earlier provider-projection head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `git diff --check`. Earlier on this PR: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: focused cron proof passed 2 shards with 101 tests after the schema-instance isolation commit; earlier focused provider proof passed 3 shards with 114 tests; prompt snapshots are current; remote `pnpm check:changed` completed successfully in run `run_4415ced3845b`.

Observed result after fix: projected cron schemas expose provider-compatible plain `string`/`array`/`enum` shapes while raw tool validation keeps `agentId`, `sessionKey`, and `payload.toolsAllow` as `null` when the schema accepts clear sentinels, and each cron tool instance receives independent nested schema objects.

What was not tested: live Telegram or full assistant-turn E2E; this PR is limited to runtime schema projection, argument validation, and cron schema-instance isolation contracts.

## Current CI note

The failed rollup on `895d50f124c4e4568c49ff2dc1a6bde1ef9fb57c` exposed branch-owned cron schema poisoning from shared `CronToolSchema` mutation; this is fixed by `15c66cfee5d`. The same run also reported an unrelated additional-runtime-topology issue in `src/channels/message/types.ts`, outside this PR.
target.author: vincentkoc
